results.title: Part-Time Student – Software Engineer – Moline, IL – Career Advising & Professional Development | MIT
highlights:
# Part-Time Student – Software Engineer – Moline, IL
...
As a Part Time Student for John Deere World Headquarters located in Moline, IL, you will:
...
- Design, implement, and operationalize systems using large language models (LLMs) and multimodal models,
...
- Build and integrate LLM-powered features, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agent frameworks, tool use, and conversational interfaces.
- Evaluate and implement open-source and commercial foundation models (text, code, image, multimodal).
- Develop embedding pipelines, vector search, and hybrid search strategies.
- Must be able to commute to the work location on scheduled workdays.
...
What Makes You Stand Out
...
- Familiarity with orchestration frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, or LlamaIndex.
- Proficiency in Python (preferred) and Java, with hands-on experience in LLM workflows.
- Familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
- Strong software engineering fundamentals in distributed systems, API development, and performance optimization.
- Experience building microservices, event-driven systems, or real-time inference services.
- Strong understanding of CI/CD, testing strategies for AI systems, and observability tools

highlights:
Data Engineer @John Deere
...
Data Engineer at [John Deere](https://www.linkedin.com/company/john-deere)
...
Hi, I am a Data Engineer at Deere working on building data products and integrating AI LLMs into our datasets. My previous roles included a rotation at John Deere Financial building and enhancing our credit decision models, and a rotation on the computer vision robotics team developing computer vision models for Deere's object detection systems. I finished my Master's degree in Data Science at Indiana University in 2023 and graduated from Purdue University with a degree in Data Science, Economics Honors, and Statistics in 2022. My past research experiences with data have shown me how powerful data analytics can be. Our society relies heavily on understanding how to process and use big data. From helping solve the coronavirus pandemic to simply buying groceries, the structure of these processes is built on data. I have found my passion in exploring and analyzing the world as a data scientist. I believe I can make important contributions to humanity as I progress through my career, and I look forward to working with companies that can help me accomplish this goal.
...
#### Data Engineer (Current)
...
#### Data Scientist - DADP
...
I collaborated with engineers, business stakeholders, UX designers, and clients to develop supervised classification models with the goal of grouping Deere's machine notifications via text analysis. This project assists in Deere's client webpage (OpsCenter), where clients can view machine status and notifications I used many natural language processing (NLP) techniques and machine learning algorithms to build my models. These models included SGD classification and multi-label classification. I was able to achieve upwards of 95% accuracy on predicting notification categories. The models are being put into use to allow easier functionality of notifications on OpsCenter. Show less

You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact.
